verses.commentary: Jacob breaks the paralysis. His sons are frozen. Why just stare at each other? Famine requires action.
Go buy. Survival imperative.
Not all eleven. One is missing.
Joseph's brother. Overprotective after losing Joseph.
Desperate times.
Joseph was governor. Bowed down. Faces to ground. The dream fulfilled (37:7). Dramatic irony.
Joseph recognizes them instantly. He knew this moment would come. The harsh speech and questions are tests, not cruelty. He must know: have they changed?
Power imbalance.
Joseph connects moment to boyhood dreams.
No, my lord. My lord. Your servants. Like Joseph predicted they would (37:8).
Ironic given what they did to Joseph.
Maintains pressure.
Their euphemism. How would Joseph feel hearing this?
You are spies! Persistent accusation.
As Pharaoh lives. Demands Benjamin.
If you are not, then as surely as Pharaoh lives, you are spies! Send one to get brother. Rest in prison. Test your words. Prison threat. Test their claims.
Three days. Joseph imprisons them.
I fear God. Claims to fear God. Will they abandon one brother like they abandoned him?
If honest. Reverses plan.
Must bring youngest. So words verified. May not die. Condition set. Benjamin must come. They agree.
He pleaded. We wouldn't listen. Guilt surfaces. They connect current suffering to past sin. Conscience awakens.
Didn't I tell you not to sin? You wouldn't listen. He tried to save Joseph (37:21-22). Divine justice.
He was using interpreter.
Turned away. Began to weep. Joseph's emotion. Composes himself. Takes Simeon. Why Simeon? Possibly ringleader after Reuben.
Give provisions. Generous treatment.
Loaded grain. Left.
Stopped for night. Discovery. Shock.
Terror replaces relief. They see God's hand pursuing them. The money isn't kindness but judgment. Twenty years later, Egypt's dungeon reminds them of a pit in Canaan.
Report to father.
Treated as spies. Recount accusation.
But we said: We are honest, not spies.
Twelve brothers.
Take food. Go. Joseph's conditions repeated.
Bring youngest. You can trade.
Emptying sacks. Were frightened. Fear multiplies. How can they explain this?
Everything is against me! Jacob said: You deprived me. Joseph no more. Simeon no more. Everything against me! Jacob's despair. But actually God is working for him.
Entrust to me. I'll bring him. Extreme guarantee.
His brother dead. Jacob refuses. Benjamin is the only one left of Rachel's sons (in his mind). Won't risk him.
